content: If we frame general reinforcement learning's reward optimization paradigm as a blank exploratory system, the 2025 iteration of the ICM curiosity mechanism can be reinterpreted as a self-generating "reward backbone" that lets RL agents avoid converging to local optimal traps even when no predefined extrinsic reward is available for long-horizon tasks. This enables fully autonomous open-ended RL agents that can evolve their own goals instead of only pursuing human-specified objectives.
